tables.name: persons
tables.description: Pipedrive-style person candidates from Salesforce Contact. Join account on AccountId for organization name when needed.

columns.name: sf_contact_id
columns.description: Salesforce Contact Id (preserve for traceability and idempotent load later).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Full display name — trim; combine FirstName and LastName with a single space.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Primary email; lowercased and trimmed (use Email if present).

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Preferred phone — Phone or MobilePhone.

columns.name: org_name
columns.description: Account name looked up via Contact.AccountId = Account.Id (staging table account).

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map from OwnerId if you can coerce to integer, otherwise null.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Contact.CreatedDate if available.

tables.name: organizations
tables.description: Pipedrive-style organization candidates from Salesforce Account.

columns.name: sf_account_id
columns.description: Salesforce Account Id (preserve for traceability).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Account Name; trimmed.

columns.name: address
columns.description: Billing address summary — concatenate BillingStreet, BillingCity, BillingState, BillingPostalCode, BillingCountry with commas/spaces.

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map OwnerId when possible.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Account.CreatedDate if present.

column_notes.explanation: Directly mapped as the primary Salesforce identifier for traceability.
Combined using CONCAT_WS with a single space, then trimmed to remove surrounding whitespace.
Normalized by trimming whitespace and lowercasing to ensure consistent string matching.
Prioritizes mobile_phone over phone using COALESCE as the preferred contact number.
Resolved via LEFT JOIN on Contact.AccountId = Account.Id to attach the parent organization name.
Safely coerced to integer only when the string contains purely digits, otherwise defaults to NULL to avoid casting errors.
Directly mapped from the contact record creation timestamp.

tables.reasoning: The model joins the contact and account tables to enrich person records with organization names. String normalization is handled using standard PostgreSQL functions (TRIM, LOWER) as requested, while a regex check safely filters numeric OwnerIds before casting to integer, preventing conversion errors on non-numeric Salesforce IDs. Soft-deleted contacts are excluded to maintain data quality.

column_notes.explanation: Directly mapped from the Salesforce Account Id to preserve traceability for subsequent Pipedrive syncs.
Trimmed account name to remove surrounding whitespace and ensure clean text output.
Concatenated billing location fields using concat_ws with ', ' delimiter, safely skipping nulls or empty strings to build a normalized address summary.
Conditionally coerced to integer only when the source string contains pure digits; defaults to null for standard alphanumeric Salesforce IDs as instructed.
Mapped directly from the Account creation timestamp to serve as the organization record addition time.

tables.reasoning: I mapped the raw Salesforce Account fields directly to the target `organizations` schema, applying `trim` for cleanliness and `concat_ws` to safely assemble the billing address while skipping missing components. Owner IDs are strictly coerced to integers only when numeric, defaulting to null per the spec since they typically contain alphanumeric Salesforce prefixes. The model uses standard PostgreSQL syntax and references the correct raw source slug for dbt lineage tracking.

column_notes.explanation: Preserves the Salesforce Contact ID as text for traceability and idempotent loads.
Combines first and last names with a single space, handling NULLs via COALESCE and trimming whitespace.
Lowercased and trimmed to ensure consistent email formatting for matching.
Prefers the primary phone number, falling back to mobile when unavailable.
Left-joins the Account table on contact.account_id to resolve the organization name from Salesforce data.
Coerces the owner ID string to an integer as requested for the Pipedrive placeholder field.
Directly maps the Salesforce contact creation timestamp to track when the person record was added.

tables.reasoning: The model joins the Contact and Account sources on their respective IDs to attach the organization name. Standard PostgreSQL string functions (TRIM, LOWER, COALESCE) normalize names, emails, and phones according to the target spec. OwnerId is cast to integer as instructed, and timestamps are mapped directly from the contact lifecycle fields.

column_notes.explanation: Directly cast from Salesforce Account Id string to text for traceability.
Trimmed account name to remove leading/trailing whitespace as required by target spec.
Concatenated billing address fields using CONCAT_WS to safely ignore NULLs and join with commas/spaces.
Safely coerced OwnerId string to integer only when it matches a numeric pattern; otherwise returns NULL per spec.
Mapped from CreatedDate, cast to plain timestamp to match target type.

tables.reasoning: The model maps the Salesforce Account raw table directly into the target `organizations` schema. Address fields are safely concatenated using `CONCAT_WS` to handle NULLs gracefully. Owner IDs are conditionally cast to integers only when purely numeric, preventing runtime cast errors while satisfying the 'otherwise null' requirement. All string normalizations follow the explicit trimming and casting instructions.

column_notes.explanation: Directly preserved as text for traceability and idempotent downstream loads.
Concatenated with a single space using CONCAT_WS (ignores NULLs), then trimmed to remove leading/trailing whitespace.
Trimmed and lowercased to standardize email formatting for matching and deduplication.
Preferred phone number; falls back to mobile_phone if primary is null/empty, with both trimmed.
Left-joined from the account staging table on Contact.AccountId = Account.Id; trimmed for cleanliness.
Attempted safe cast to integer using a numeric regex check. Salesforce IDs contain letters, so this safely returns NULL when coercion is impossible, preventing runtime cast errors.
Directly mapped from the Contact CreatedDate field and cast to standard timestamp.

tables.reasoning: The query joins the contact table with the account table on AccountId to enrich person records with organization names. Normalization steps include trimming names, lowercasing emails, and coalescing phone fields as specified. A safe integer casting pattern is applied to owner_id to gracefully handle non-numeric Salesforce IDs without causing model failures. All output columns strictly match the target schema types and constraints.

column_notes.explanation: Mapped directly from Account.id and cast to text for traceability.
Extracted from Account.name and trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace as specified.
Concatenated billing location fields using CONCAT_WS to safely join with commas while automatically skipping NULL values.
Coerced to integer for Pipedrive mapping; empty strings are handled via NULLIF, non-numeric values default to NULL.
Directly mapped from Account.created_date to capture record creation timestamp.

tables.reasoning: The model filters out soft-deleted accounts and extracts the core organizational fields. PostgreSQL's CONCAT_WS is used to construct the billing address safely, avoiding null-related concatenation issues. Owner IDs are cast to integer for downstream compatibility, with empty values explicitly converted to NULL per specification.

column_notes.explanation: Preserved directly from the Contact record for traceability and idempotent loads.
Concatenated with a space, trimmed, and title-cased using INITCAP to match Pipedrive display name format.
Lowercased and trimmed to normalize email addresses for consistent lookup/matching.
Prefers primary phone, falls back to mobile if null, then trimmed of whitespace.
Looked up via LEFT JOIN on AccountId = Id; null when contact has no associated account.
Safely coerced to integer where the Salesforce ID contains only digits, otherwise null to prevent cast errors.
Extracted creation timestamp from Contact.CreatedDate and cast to plain timestamp.

tables.reasoning: Joined the raw contact table with the account table on the foreign key to resolve organization names. Applied PostgreSQL-standard text normalization (trim, lower, initcap) as requested, and used a safe regex-based integer cast for owner_id to handle Salesforce's typical alphanumeric IDs without runtime errors. Filtered soft-deleted contacts to ensure clean person candidates.

column_notes.explanation: Direct mapping from Salesforce Account Id for traceability.
Account Name trimmed to remove surrounding whitespace per target spec.
Concatenated using CONCAT_WS which safely skips NULLs and handles comma-space delimiters automatically.
Safe coercion to integer using regex guard; returns NULL for non-numeric OwnerIds (e.g., Salesforce alphanumeric IDs).
Direct mapping from Account creation timestamp.

tables.reasoning: Straightforward 1:1 mapping with minor normalization. Used CONCAT_WS for robust address concatenation that gracefully handles nulls and avoids stray delimiters. Applied a regex-based safe cast for owner_id to comply with PostgreSQL's strict typing without relying on non-standard TRY_CAST functions. Trimmed the account name as specified.
